id.description: Universally unique identifier of the event. To allow for reliable retransmission, only the earliest received event of a given UUID is considered valid by the server and subsequent are ignored.

category.description: The feature category to which the feedback applies. These should be broad product areas such as Announcements, Answers, Search, etc. rather than specific components or UI treatments within those areas.

trackingTokens.description: A list of server-generated trackingTokens to which this event applies.

event.description: The action the user took within a Glean client with respect to the object referred to by the given `trackingToken`.

x-enumDescriptions.CLICK: The object's primary link was clicked with the intent to view its full representation. Depending on the object type, this may imply an external navigation or navigating to a new page or view within the Glean app.
x-enumDescriptions.CONTAINER_CLICK: A link to the object's parent container (e.g. the folder in which it's located) was clicked.
x-enumDescriptions.COPY_LINK: The user copied a link to the primary link.
x-enumDescriptions.DISMISS: The user dismissed the object such that it was hidden from view.
x-enumDescriptions.DOWNVOTE: The user gave feedback that the object was not useful.
x-enumDescriptions.EMAIL: The user attempted to send an email.
x-enumDescriptions.EXECUTE: The user executed the object (e.g. ran a workflow).
x-enumDescriptions.FIRST_TOKEN: The first token of a streaming response is received.
x-enumDescriptions.FOCUS_IN: The user clicked into an interactive element, e.g. the search box.
x-enumDescriptions.LAST_TOKEN: The final token of a streaming response is received.
x-enumDescriptions.MANUAL_FEEDBACK: The user submitted textual manual feedback regarding the object.
x-enumDescriptions.MANUAL_FEEDBACK_SIDE_BY_SIDE: The user submitted comparative feedback for multiple side-by-side implementations.
x-enumDescriptions.FEEDBACK_TIME_SAVED: The user submitted feedback about time saved by an agent or workflow.
x-enumDescriptions.MARK_AS_READ: The user explicitly marked the content as read.
x-enumDescriptions.MESSAGE: The user attempted to send a message using their default messaing app.
x-enumDescriptions.MIDDLE_CLICK: The user middle clicked the object's primary link with the intent to open its full representation in a new tab.
x-enumDescriptions.PAGE_BLUR: The user puts a page out of focus but keeps it in the background.
x-enumDescriptions.PAGE_FOCUS: The user puts a page in focus, meaning it is the first to receive keyboard events.
x-enumDescriptions.PAGE_LEAVE: The user leaves a page and it is unloaded (by clicking a link, closing the tab/window, etc).
x-enumDescriptions.PREVIEW: The user clicked the object's inline preview affordance.
x-enumDescriptions.RIGHT_CLICK: The user right clicked the object's primary link. This may indicate an intent to open it in a new tab or copy it.
x-enumDescriptions.SECTION_CLICK: The user clicked a link to a subsection of the primary object.
x-enumDescriptions.SEEN: The user has likely seen the object (e.g. took action to make the object visible within the user's viewport).
x-enumDescriptions.SELECT: The user explicitly selected something, eg. a chat response variant they prefer.
x-enumDescriptions.SHARE: The user shared the object with another user.
x-enumDescriptions.SHOW_MORE: The user clicked the object's show more affordance.
x-enumDescriptions.UPVOTE: The user gave feedback that the object was useful.
x-enumDescriptions.VIEW: The object was visible within the user's viewport.
x-enumDescriptions.VISIBLE: The object was visible within the user's viewport.

x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.CLICK: The object's primary link was clicked with the intent to view its full representation. Depending on the object type, this may imply an external navigation or navigating to a new page or view within the Glean app.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.CONTAINER_CLICK: A link to the object's parent container (e.g. the folder in which it's located) was clicked.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.COPY_LINK: The user copied a link to the primary link.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.DISMISS: The user dismissed the object such that it was hidden from view.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.DOWNVOTE: The user gave feedback that the object was not useful.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.EMAIL: The user attempted to send an email.

x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.FOCUS_IN: The user clicked into an interactive element, e.g. the search box.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.LAST_TOKEN: The final token of a streaming response is received.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.MANUAL_FEEDBACK: The user submitted textual manual feedback regarding the object.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.MANUAL_FEEDBACK_SIDE_BY_SIDE: The user submitted comparative feedback for multiple side-by-side implementations.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.FEEDBACK_TIME_SAVED: The user submitted feedback about time saved by an agent or workflow.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.MARK_AS_READ: The user explicitly marked the content as read.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.MESSAGE: The user attempted to send a message using their default messaing app.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.MIDDLE_CLICK: The user middle clicked the object's primary link with the intent to open its full representation in a new tab.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.PAGE_BLUR: The user puts a page out of focus but keeps it in the background.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.PAGE_FOCUS: The user puts a page in focus, meaning it is the first to receive keyboard events.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.PAGE_LEAVE: The user leaves a page and it is unloaded (by clicking a link, closing the tab/window, etc).
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.PREVIEW: The user clicked the object's inline preview affordance.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.RIGHT_CLICK: The user right clicked the object's primary link. This may indicate an intent to open it in a new tab or copy it.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.SECTION_CLICK: The user clicked a link to a subsection of the primary object.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.SEEN: The user has likely seen the object (e.g. took action to make the object visible within the user's viewport).
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.SELECT: The user explicitly selected something, eg. a chat response variant they prefer.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.SHARE: The user shared the object with another user.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.SHOW_MORE: The user clicked the object's show more affordance.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.UPVOTE: The user gave feedback that the object was useful.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.VIEW: The object was visible within the user's viewport.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.VISIBLE: The object was visible within the user's viewport.

position.description: Position of the element in the case that the client controls order (such as feed and autocomplete).

payload.description: For type MANUAL_FEEDBACK, contains string of user feedback. For autocomplete, partial query string. For feed, string of user feedback in addition to manual feedback signals extracted from all suggested content.

sessionTrackingToken.description: A unique token for this session. A new session (and token) is created when the user issues a request from a new tab or when our server hasn't seen activity for more than 10 minutes from a tab.

tabId.description: A unique id for all requests a user makes from a given tab, no matter how far apart. A new tab id is only generated when a user issues a request from a new tab.

lastSeen.description: The last time the server saw this token.

lastQuery.description: The last query seen by the server.

timestamp.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp when the event occured.

userID.description: An opaque user ID for the claimed authority (i.e., the actas param, or the origid if actas is not specified).

origID.description: An opaque user ID for the authenticated user (ignores actas).

pathname.description: The path the client was at when the feedback event triggered.

channels.description: Where the feedback will be sent, e.g. to Glean, the user's company, or both. If no channels are specified, feedback will go only to Glean.

url.description: The URL the client was at when the feedback event triggered.

uiTree.description: The UI element tree associated with the event, if any.

uiElement.description: The UI element associated with the event, if any.

email.description: The email address of the user who submitted the Feedback.event.MANUAL_FEEDBACK event.

source.description: The source associated with the Feedback.event.MANUAL_FEEDBACK event.

issue.description: The issue the user indicated in the feedback.

issues.description: The issue(s) the user indicated in the feedback.

imageUrls.description: URLs of images uploaded by user when providing feedback

query.description: The query associated with the Feedback.event.MANUAL_FEEDBACK event.

obscuredQuery.description: The query associated with the Feedback.event.MANUAL_FEEDBACK event, but obscured such that the vowels are replaced with special characters. For search feedback events only.

activeTab.description: Which tabs the user had chosen at the time of the Feedback.event.MANUAL_FEEDBACK event. For search feedback events only.

comments.description: The comments users can optionally add to the Feedback.event.MANUAL_FEEDBACK events.

searchResults.description: The array of search result Glean Document IDs, ordered by top to bottom result.

previousMessages.description: The array of previous messages in a chat session, ordered by oldest to newest.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp in millis for the chat request.

agent.description: Either DEFAULT (company knowledge) or GPT (world knowledge).

userQuery.description: Initial query entered by the user.

searchQuery.description: Search query performed by the agent.

resultDocuments.description: List of documents read by the agent.

chatTranscript.description: Array of previous request/response exchanges, ordered by oldest to newest.

numQueriesFromFirstRun.description: How many times this query has been run in the past.

vote.description: The vote associated with the Feedback.event.MANUAL_FEEDBACK event.

rating.description: A rating associated with the user feedback. The value will be between one and the maximum given by ratingScale, inclusive.

ratingKey.description: A description of the rating that contextualizes how it appeared to the user, e.g. "satisfied".

ratingScale.description: The scale of comparison for a rating associated with the feedback. Rating values start from one and go up to the maximum specified by ratingScale. For example, a five-option satisfaction rating will have a ratingScale of 5 and a thumbs-up/thumbs-down rating will have a ratingScale of 2.

email.description: The email address of the user who submitted the side-by-side feedback.

source.description: The source associated with the side-by-side feedback event.

query.description: The query or prompt that was evaluated across multiple implementations.

implementations.description: Array of implementations that were compared side-by-side.

implementationId.description: Unique identifier for this implementation variant.

implementationName.description: Human-readable name for this implementation (e.g., "Variant A", "GPT-4", "Claude").

searchParams.description: The search/chat parameters used for this implementation.

response.description: The full response generated by this implementation.

responseMetadata.description: Metadata about the response (e.g., latency, token count).

latencyMs.description: Time taken to generate the response in milliseconds.

tokenCount.description: Number of tokens in the response.

evaluationSessionId.description: Unique identifier for this evaluation session to group related feedback events.

implementationId.description: The ID of the implementation this specific feedback event is for.

vote.description: The vote for this specific implementation.

comments.description: Specific feedback comments for this implementation.

isExplicit.description: The confidence of the user seeing the object is high because they explicitly interacted with it e.g. answer impression in SERP with additional user interaction.

docId.description: Unique Glean Document ID of the associated document.

source.description: Where the feedback of the workflow originated from

applicationId.description: The application ID of the client that sent the feedback event.

agentId.description: The agent ID of the client that sent the feedback event.
